title: crotalaria arborescens
intro: Crotalaria arborescens, also known as the 'tree cassia', is a plant species native to tropical regions of Asia and Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Crotalaria arborescens include the treatment of various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a diuretic and a laxative. In terms of herbal preparations, Crotalaria arborescens is often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be consumed orally to reap its medicinal benefits.
